title: linum hirsutum subsp. platyphyllum
intro: Linum hirsutum subsp. platyphyllum, also known as the hairy flax or woolly flax, is a plant species in the flax family (Linaceae) characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the skin. The health benefits of Linum hirsutum subsp. platyphyllum include its ability to calm digestive issues, reduce skin irritations, and alleviate coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
